papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.author: Vaswani
papers.abstract: We propose the Transformer, a novel architecture based entirely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolution. The Transformer achieves state-of-the-art results on machine translation benchmarks while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train.

papers.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers
papers.author: Devlin
papers.abstract: We introduce BERT, a method for pre-training deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text by jointly conditioning on both left and right context. Fine-tuned BERT achieves state-of-the-art results on eleven NLP benchmarks.

papers.title: Language Models are Unsupervised Multitask Learners
papers.author: Radford
papers.abstract: We demonstrate that language models trained on a large corpus of web text can perform downstream tasks without explicit supervision. GPT-2 with 1.5 billion parameters achieves state-of-the-art on seven out of eight language modeling benchmarks in a zero-shot setting.

papers.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
papers.author: Brown
papers.abstract: We show that scaling up language models greatly improves task-agnostic few-shot performance. GPT-3 with 175 billion parameters achieves strong performance on many NLP tasks using only a few demonstrations provided in the prompt context.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
papers.author: Kaplan
papers.abstract: We study empirical scaling laws for language model performance on the cross-entropy loss. Performance depends strongly on scale, with smooth power law relationships between model size, dataset size, and the amount of compute used for training.

papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
papers.author: Lewis
papers.abstract: We introduce RAG, a model that combines pre-trained parametric memory with a non-parametric retrieval component for language generation. RAG models generate more specific, diverse, and factual text than purely parametric seq2seq models.

papers.title: Dense Passage Retrieval for Open-Domain Question Answering
papers.author: Karpukhin
papers.abstract: We show that retrieval using dense vector representations alone can substantially outperform sparse retrieval methods like TF-IDF and BM25 for open-domain question answering. Our simple dual-encoder framework significantly improves QA accuracy.

papers.title: REALM: Retrieval-Augmented Language Model Pre-Training
papers.author: Guu
papers.abstract: We augment language model pre-training with a learned textual knowledge retriever. REALM learns to retrieve documents that help predict masked tokens and achieves state-of-the-art results on open-domain question answering benchmarks.

papers.title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique
papers.author: Asai
papers.abstract: We introduce Self-RAG, a framework that trains a language model to adaptively retrieve passages and generate text while reflecting on its own output. Self-RAG significantly outperforms existing RAG approaches and LLMs with retrieval on diverse tasks.

papers.title: From Local to Global: A Graph RAG Approach to Query-Focused Summarization
papers.author: Edge
papers.abstract: We propose Graph RAG, which uses LLM-generated knowledge graphs to enable both local and global search over large text corpora. Graph RAG substantially improves the comprehensiveness and diversity of generated answers for global sensemaking questions.

papers.title: Unifying Large Language Models and Knowledge Graphs: A Roadmap
papers.author: Pan
papers.abstract: We present a roadmap for unifying large language models and knowledge graphs, covering KG-enhanced LLMs, LLM-augmented KGs, and synergized LLMs plus KGs. We discuss key challenges and future research directions for this integration.

papers.title: KG-RAG: Bridging the Gap Between Knowledge and Creativity
papers.author: Soman
papers.abstract: We propose KG-RAG, which integrates biomedical knowledge graphs with retrieval-augmented generation to enhance LLM reasoning about medical questions. KG-RAG improves zero-shot performance on medical QA benchmarks without requiring domain-specific fine-tuning.

papers.title: Think-on-Graph: Deep and Responsible Reasoning of LLMs on Knowledge Graphs
papers.author: Sun
papers.abstract: We propose Think-on-Graph, a framework that enables LLMs to perform beam search on knowledge graphs for faithful and responsible reasoning. The approach grounds LLM reasoning in structured knowledge, significantly reducing hallucination.

papers.title: Graph RAG: Unlocking LLM Discovery on Narrative Private Data
papers.author: Edge
papers.abstract: We explore how graph-based text indexing approaches can help LLMs reason over private narrative datasets. Using community summaries from knowledge graphs enables answering global questions that are impossible with standard vector-based RAG.

papers.title: Efficient and Robust Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search Using HNSW Graphs
papers.author: Malkov
papers.abstract: We propose HNSW, a graph-based algorithm for approximate nearest neighbor search that uses hierarchical navigable small world graphs. HNSW provides state-of-the-art performance in terms of speed-recall tradeoff across multiple benchmarks.

papers.title: Sentence-BERT: Sentence Embeddings using Siamese BERT-Networks
papers.author: Reimers
papers.abstract: We develop Sentence-BERT, a modification of BERT using siamese and triplet network structures to derive semantically meaningful sentence embeddings. This enables efficient semantic similarity comparison via cosine similarity for large-scale applications.

papers.title: Text Embeddings by Weakly-Supervised Contrastive Pre-training
papers.author: Wang
papers.abstract: We present E5, a family of text embedding models trained with contrastive pre-training using weak supervision from large-scale text pairs. E5 achieves strong performance on a wide range of embedding and retrieval benchmarks.

papers.title: Matryoshka Representation Learning
papers.author: Kusupati
papers.abstract: We propose Matryoshka Representation Learning, which encodes information at different granularities to produce embeddings that are useful at multiple dimensions. This allows flexible deployment without retraining for different resource constraints.
